sections.paragraph:
Ἀγρίππας, α, ὁ name of various political figures: Agrippa (lit. [Diod S 12, 30, 1; ParJer 3:14; 21:5; 22; GrBar ins 2; Philo, Joseph.]; ins, pap, coins B-D-F §54; 55, 1)

① Herod Agrippa I (10 b.c.–44 a.d.), son of Aristobulus, grandson of Herod the Great; ruler first (37) of Gaulanitis, Trachonitis, Batanaea, Panias, then (39) also of Abilene, Galilee, and Perea, finally (41) also of Judea and Samaria; called Herod in Ac 12:1ff.—Schürer I 442–54 (sources and lit.); ESchwartz, NGG 1907, 263ff; Rosenberg in Pauly-W. X/1, 1917, 143–46; Meyer III 42f; 167f; 541f.

② Herod Agrippa II (28–92/93 a.d.), son of 1, ruled over the tetrarchy of Philip fr. 53 to his death. For his part in Paul’s trial s. Ac 25:13ff (he is mentioned 25:13, 22–24, 26; 26:1f, 7 v.l., 19, 27f, 32).—Schürer I 471–83 (sources and lit.); ESchwartz, NGG 1907, 263ff; HTajra, The Trial of St. Paul ’89, 152–71; Rosenberg in Pauly-W. X/1, 1917, 146–50.—On both s. WOtto and HWillrich on Ἡρῴδης, beg.

③ Name of Rom. governor AcPt Ox 849 recto, 25f.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

NGG NGG = Nachrichten der Gesellschaft der Wissenschaften zu/in Göttingen, Philologisch-historische Klasse—List 6

Pauly-W Pauly-W = AP./GW., Real-Encyclopädie der klassischen Altertumswissenschaft—List 6

Meyer Meyer = EM., Ursprung und Anfänge des Christentums—List 6

  1. fr. = from

beg. beg. = beginning

AcPt Ox 849 AcPt Ox 849 = Acts of Peter, POxy 849—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 15
